reason: The prompt cleanly targets a single integrated engineering judgment: timing of release vs TR trajectory, encapsulation vs ionic-conductivity penalty, and a specific delay-induced failure condition. It offers strong discrimination because it forces boundary-aware reasoning (still helpful after heat generation begins, but potentially harmful if separator-shrinkage/shorting precedes release) while constraining overclaiming. Minor risk remains that, as a short-answer item, grading consistency depends on whether partial mechanistic descriptions are deemed sufficient without the exemplar numeric anchoring.
